_readme.description: HomeschoolIQ — Seed source list for the scraper pipeline.

methodology_grade_values.A: Randomized or controlled study — documented control group
methodology_grade_values.C: Self-selected sample, no controls, or advocacy-funded without disclosure
methodology_grade_values.D: Anecdotal, opinion piece, or undisclosed methodology

scrape_mode_values.pdf: pdfplumber extraction — defer until HTML sources are complete

_readme.balance_rule: Minimum 2 sources per tier. No single tier exceeds 40% of active sources. Positive, critical, and neutral voices in deliberate proportion.

current_tier_counts.note: Government is at the 40% ceiling (4/10). Do not add government sources without removing or deactivating another. validate_raw.py asserts this balance rule on every run.

sources.title: NCES — Homeschooling in the United States
sources.notes: Federal survey data on homeschool enrollment and demographics. Primary source for enrollment trend stats.

sources.title: NCES — Parent and Family Involvement Survey
sources.notes: Reasons families choose homeschooling — safety, religion, dissatisfaction with schools. Useful for demographic and motivation breakdowns.

sources.title: NCES — Per Pupil Expenditure in Public Schools
sources.notes: Primary source for Cost category. Public per-pupil expenditure figures for comparison against family homeschool costs. Pairs with family cost estimates from NHERI and HSLDA.

sources.title: Census — Household Pulse Survey, Homeschooling
sources.notes: COVID-era homeschool growth data. Key source for post-2020 era enrollment surge stats.

sources.title: NHERI — Academic Achievement Research
sources.notes: Brian Ray's aggregated research findings. Widely cited but uses self-selected samples — selection_bias_flag should default to 1 for most stats from this source.

sources.title: NHERI — Social-Emotional Development Research
sources.notes: Social-emotional and civic outcomes research. Key source for semantic_cluster tagging. Apply selection_bias_flag — self-selected sample.

sources.title: PLOS ONE — Social Development in Homeschooled vs. Traditionally Schooled Children
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed social development study with documented methodology. Primary source for semantic_cluster stats. Provides the controlled comparison the socialization argument requires to be credible.

sources.title: HSLDA — Homeschool Academic Stats
sources.notes: Advocacy organization with institutional interest in positive findings. Stats valuable but require selection_bias_flag and should be weighed against Tier A/B sources.

sources.title: Education Week — Homeschooling Coverage
sources.notes: Primary critical coverage source. Captures concerns, negative findings, and policy debates not represented in advocacy sources. Essential for balance.

sources.title: Education Week — Homeschool Oversight and Abuse Coverage
sources.notes: Covers regulatory gaps and documented abuse cases. This is the Critique category source. Uncomfortable but necessary for intellectual honesty.
